source.update_note: Could not fetch source page. Data is from the Hunting Seasons page and Small Game Guide.

sub_seasons.name: Youth Pheasant, Quail & Partridge Season
sub_seasons.note: Youth ages 15 and under only. Accompanying adult (19+) must be licensed; only one adult per youth may hunt.

species.name: Prairie Grouse
species.hunting_units: Statewide (special permit required east of U.S. Highway 81 — see key notes)
species.bag_limit: 3 daily (combined Sharp-tailed Grouse and Greater Prairie-chicken)

licenses.name: Hunt Permit (Resident, age 16+)
licenses.covers: All small game including pheasant, quail, partridge, prairie grouse, squirrel, rabbit, and furbearers

purchase_urls.label: Nebraska Game & Parks Online License Portal
Nebraska Game & Parks Online License Portal

licenses.name: Nonresident Annual Hunt Permit (age 16+)
licenses.covers: All small game including upland birds

purchase_urls.label: Nebraska Game & Parks Online License Portal

licenses.name: Nonresident 2-Day Hunt Permit (age 16+)
licenses.covers: All small game including upland birds (2 consecutive days)

purchase_urls.label: Nebraska Game & Parks Online License Portal

licenses.name: Habitat Stamp
licenses.covers: Required annually for all hunters pursuing small game (with select exemptions)

purchase_urls.label: Nebraska Game & Parks Online License Portal

key_notes.title: Prairie grouse (sharp-tailed grouse & greater prairie-chicken) require a special permit when hunting east of U.S. Highway 81.

sources.label: Nebraska Game & Parks — Hunting Seasons
sources.evidence: Prairie grouse: Sept. 1, 2026 - Jan. 31, 2027. *special permit required east of U.S. 81

sources.label: 2025-2026 Small Game and Waterfowl Guide — Prairie Grouse Section
sources.evidence: A special permit is required to hunt prairie grouse east of U.S. Highway 81.

key_notes.title: Youth Pheasant, Quail & Partridge season runs two days before the regular opener; youth ages 15 and under only.

sources.label: Nebraska Game & Parks — Upland Hunting
sources.evidence: Nebraska's youth-only pheasant, quail and partridge season is Oct. 24-25, 2026; only youth ages 15 and under are allowed to hunt on these days.

sources.label: NGPC — Youth Pheasant Season News
sources.evidence: The accompanying adult must be a licensed hunter aged 19 or older and only one adult per youth will be allowed to hunt.

key_notes.title: A Habitat Stamp ($25) is required for all hunters pursuing small game, with select exemptions.

sources.label: NGPC — Permit & Stamp Requirements
sources.evidence: A Habitat Stamp is required of all hunters pursuing any small game, with select exemptions.

key_notes.title: Only male pheasants (roosters with visible spurs or plumage) may be taken.

sources.label: Nebraska Game & Parks — Hunting Seasons
sources.evidence: Pheasant: 3 per day (12 in possession) — roosters only.

key_notes.title: Prairie grouse daily bag limit is 3 birds total in aggregate (sharp-tailed grouse and greater prairie-chicken combined), with a possession limit of 3.

sources.label: eRegulations — Nebraska Bag Limits
sources.evidence: Prairie Grouse: 3 per day (3 in possession in aggregate)
